free_text: Cross sections from 800 MeV proton irradiation of
terbium
Experiment performed using 800 MeV
1H+ ions at the LANSCE accelerator facility in the
Weapons Neutron Research (WNR) Blue Room.
Natural Terbium foils with diameter of 24.7mm,
thickness of 80.4 mg/cm2 and purity of 99.9%. Target
foils were sandwiched between single 25 mu-m thick
Kapton tape, which served as a catcher for recoil
ions.Two target foil stacks were irradiated in the
measurement of the data reported here. The first
stack included only two terbium foils and accepted a
beam current of 49 +- 3 nA for approximately 0.5
hours. The second stack, comprised of 10 terbium
foils, was irradiated for 10.4 +- 0.1 hours with an
average beam current of 50 +- 4 nA
The HPGe detector used to assay the foils was a
p-type Al-window ORTEC GEM detector with a relative
efficiency at 1333 keV of about 10% and a measured
FWHM at 1333 keV of 1.99 keV.
The activity at the end of bombardment of each
isotope of interest was determined by fitting of
its decay curve.
Gamma energies and intensities were taken by authors
from NNDC online databases.
Uncertainties in linear regressions fitted parameters
were computed from covariance matrices as the standard
error in the activity extrapolated to the end of
bombardment.
Total uncertainty
Systematic uncertainty in detector calibration
and geometry
Syst. uncertainty in target foil dimensions
Systematic uncertainty in proton flux
determination
Compiled by S.H.
